bead.title: Make fiz models refresh UX coordinator-aware
bead.description:
Update fiz models CLI behavior to match the approved freshness contract.

Required CLI behavior:
- fiz models returns the current cached snapshot quickly.
- If stale routing-relevant fields are present, fiz models may request a best-effort coordinated refresh through Fizeau's lock/single-flight path, but it must not start duplicate uncoordinated probe work.
- If stale routing-relevant fields remain stale, fiz models prints a concise stale-data hint: run fiz models --refresh or start/configure a DDx server freshness heartbeat.
- fiz models --refresh performs a bounded foreground refresh of routing-relevant stale fields before printing.
- fiz models --refresh-all performs a bounded foreground refresh of all refreshable fields before printing.
- Output marks stale/fresh/refresh_failed state clearly in JSON and human views.

In scope: agentcli/cmd fiz models command flags, user-facing text, JSON fields, tests with fake coordinator.

Out of scope: implementing route scoring; implementing the low-level coordinator if the prior bead has not landed; implementing DDx server itself.
bead.acceptance:
1. go test ./agentcli ./cmd/fiz -run 'Test.*Models.*Refresh|Test.*Models.*Stale|Test.*Models.*Coordinator|Test.*Models.*Heartbeat' -count=1 passes.
2. fiz models --help documents --refresh and --refresh-all.
3. In a test with stale fields and no long-running maintainer, fiz models exits 0, does not invoke uncoordinated provider probes, and prints a hint mentioning --refresh and DDx server freshness.
4. In a test with stale fields and a fake coordinator, fiz models exits 0 and records at most one coordinated refresh request.
5. In tests, fiz models --refresh blocks until routing-relevant fake refresh completes, and --refresh-all also refreshes non-routing fields.
